perturbation.original_text: 9. Notice of Immunity . Employee is provided notice that an individual shall not be held criminally or civilly liable under any federal or state trade secret law for the disclosure of a trade secret that is made (i) in confidence to a federal, state, or local government official, either directly or indirectly, or to an attorney; and (ii) solely for the purpose of reporting or investigating a suspected violation of law; or is made in a complaint or other document filed in a lawsuit or other proceeding, if such filing is made under seal. An individual who files a lawsuit for retaliation by an employer for reporting a suspected violation of law may disclose the trade secret to the attorney of the individual and use the trade secret information in the court proceeding, if the individual (i) files any document containing the trade secret under seal; and (ii) does not disclose the trade secret, except pursuant t o court order.
perturbation.changed_text:
5. Relationships . Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be deemed to constitute either party a partner, joint venture or employee of the other party for any purpose.

9. Notice of Immunity . Employee is provided notice that an individual shall not be held criminally or civilly liable under any federal or state trade secret law for the disclosure of a trade secret that is made (i) in confidence to a federal, state, or local government official, either directly or indirectly, or to an attorney; and (ii) solely for the purpose of reporting or investigating a suspected violation of law; or is made in a complaint or other document filed in a lawsuit or other proceeding, if such filing is made under seal. An individual who files a lawsuit for retaliation by an employer for reporting a suspected violation of law may disclose the trade secret to the attorney of the individual and use the trade secret information in the court proceeding, if the individual (i) files any document containing the trade secret under seal; and (ii) does not disclose the trade secret, except pursuant t o court order.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Notice of Immunity' clause from a prominent, separate section to be embedded within the 'Relationships' section reduces its visibility and prominence. This makes it less likely that the receiving party will be aware of their immunity rights under trade secret law.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Defend Trade Secrets Act (DTSA) includes whistleblower protections for individuals who disclose trade secrets to government officials for the purpose of reporting or investigating suspected violations of law. Moving the notice of immunity to a less noticeable section contradicts the intent of the DTSA to ensure employees are aware of these protections, potentially chilling their willingness to report violations.

Exceptions to prohibitions (a) In General .-This chapter does not prohibit or create a private right of action for- (1) any otherwise lawful activity conducted by a governmental entity of the United States, a State, or a political subdivision of a State; or (2) the disclosure of a trade secret in accordance with subsection (b). (b) Immunity From Liability for Confidential Disclosure of a Trade Secret to the Gov
perturbation.justification: The Defend Trade Secrets Act (DTSA) aims to protect whistleblowers who disclose trade secrets to government officials. Moving the 'Notice of Immunity' clause, as described in the Perturbation Explanation, to a less visible section within the contract undermines the intent of the DTSA by reducing the likelihood that the employee is aware of these protections. According to the Law Explanation, this potentially chills their willingness to report violations. The scraped law snippet 1, 18 USC 1833, concerns exceptions to prohibitions related to trade secrets, and includes subsection (b) related to 'Immunity From Liability for Confidential Disclosure of a Trade Secret to the Gov'. By obscuring the notice of immunity, the modified text creates a structural flaw that makes it more difficult for employees to avail themselves of this immunity, thus contradicting the purpose of the law. The structural flaw (legal contradiction) definition specifies that a required legal disclosure placed in an irrelevant section makes it difficult for parties to locate or interpret it. This situation creates a legal contradiction because the changed text introduces a structural flaw by obscuring the notice of immunity, hindering employees' ability to understand their rights under the DTSA.
